implementation_blocked_reason: T11 (Morpheus architecture review) ACCEPTED 2026-06-01. T12 (Trinity security review) ACCEPTED 2026-06-01. Binding conditions BC-01 through BC-09 apply before T02-T04 token acquisition code ships. See .progress.md T12 section.

spfx-webpart.cache_storage: SPFx runtime managed (no localStorage by web part)

identity_invariant: userId and any body field are display/context only. Identity is established solely by the validated bearer token at the BFF.

ADR-M7-01.question: MSAL token cache storage location
ADR-M7-01.decision: Option A -- sessionStorage (not localStorage). Survives tab refresh; not cross-tab readable. localStorage prohibited. storeAuthStateInCookie: false required. Application code must not write raw token strings to any storage API.

ADR-M7-02.question: Classic loader token provider interface: pluggable vs hard-coded
ADR-M7-02.decision: Option A -- pluggable callback. Default implementation uses _spPageContextInfo.aadTokenProviderFactory. Stub provider for local mock dev. No ADAL. Token used in-memory only; never stored to localStorage/sessionStorage.

ADR-M7-03.question: Azure E2E gate timing: M7 vs separate gate
ADR-M7-03.decision: Option A -- defer to M8 (per roadmap.md). M7 stays local/mock only. Live tests require opt-in LIVE_AZURE_TESTS env flag; never in public CI defaults. OQ5 closed: gate is M8.

ADR-M7-04.question: OQ1: Optional display_name in /chat/session request body
ADR-M7-04.decision: Option B -- accept optional display_name: str | None = None. Display hint only. Not used for auth, not echoed in response, not forwarded downstream. Binding constraints documented in ADR 0009 (docs/adr/0009-m7-display-name-bff-body-field.md). OQ1 closed.

ADR-M7-05.question: OQ2: SPFx BFF token acquisition — AadHttpClient vs AadTokenProvider
ADR-M7-05.decision: Option A -- AadHttpClient via context.aadHttpClientFactory.getClient(bffResourceUri). Token abstracted from web part code; Authorization header injected automatically by SPFx runtime. AadTokenProvider rejected: exposes raw token to web part, increasing persistence/logging risk. SPHttpClient prohibited for BFF calls.

validation_commands: cd apps/spa-public-client/react-vite && npm ci && npm run build
cd apps/sharepoint-classic/chat-loader-js && npm ci && npm run lint
cd apps/spfx-webpart/identity-chat-webpart && npm ci && gulp build
public_safe_constraints: No real tenant IDs, client IDs, subscription IDs, secrets, or live tokens in any committed file.
All placeholder values use {tenant-id}, {client-id}, {bff-api-scope} brace tokens or all-zero GUIDs.
No live Azure tests in public CI. LIVE_AZURE_TESTS=true opt-in required for E2E tests.
out_of_scope_m7: .NET or Node Agent Execution Service runtime variants

azure_e2e_gate.name: M8
azure_e2e_gate.description: Dedicated post-M7 milestone gate for live Azure E2E verification: client -> APIM -> BFF -> Agent Execution Service -> MCP with real Entra tokens. Formerly referenced as M7-AzureVerify; aligned to roadmap.md which names this milestone M8.

dependencies.notes: M7 depends on M6 ACA baseline for future Azure E2E verification. All three client variants depend on the existing BFF /chat/session endpoint (M4) and JWKS validation (M1).

notes: Spec 007 created by Mouse (Frontend/SharePoint/TypeScript) as M7 spec-first gate artifact.
Implementation is BLOCKED pending T12 (Trinity) security review sign-off. T11 (Morpheus) is complete and accepted.
Backend (BFF, Agent Execution Service, MCP Protected API) unchanged in M7 except minor BFF CORS + optional display_name field.
Runtime language variants (.NET/Node Agent Execution Service) are explicitly out of M7 scope -- future milestone.
Azure E2E gate is M8 per roadmap.md (OQ5 closed). Not a M7 sub-gate. Not part of M7 implementation.
Directive from copilot-directive-20260510202925.md honored: roadmap updated to include Azure E2E gate.

T11 implementation conditions (binding on implementation tasks): (1) T00 must add 'traceparent' to BFF CORS allow_headers. (2) T01: display_name must not be echoed in session response; negative test required; max-length guard recommended. (3) T03: classic loader must not use userId as auth signal; identity invariant comment required at BFF call site.
T11 sign-off: Morpheus, Lead/Architect, 2026-06-01. Verdict: ACCEPTED. Formal ADR created: docs/adr/0009-m7-display-name-bff-body-field.md.
